scheme_name: Coaching by Private Institutes for All India Services Civil Services Examination
page_title: Coaching by Private Institutes for All India Services Civil Services Examination

details: Launched on 12th September 2013, the scheme "Coaching by Private Institutes for All India Services Civil Services Examination (Scheduled Tribes)" by the Tribal Welfare and Scheduled Caste (SC) Department, Madhya Pradesh, provides financial assistance, education, and training to Scheduled Tribe candidates preparing for the Civil Services Examination.
benefits: up to ₹2,00,000/- or actual fee (whichever is less) paid directly to the empanelled coaching institute in Delhi.
of ₹15,000/- for purchasing books, credited directly to the candidate’s account.
of ₹12,500/- for up to 18 months, based on attendance.
facilities of ₹12,500/- per month, paid directly to the candidate’s account.
eligibility: The applicant should be a native of Madhya Pradesh.
The applicant should be from a Scheduled Tribe.
The applicant should have an annual income from all sources not exceeding ₹6,00,000.
The applicant should have passed graduation.
The applicant should have been selected based on merit in Arts/Commerce/Science/Engineering/Medical groups.
The applicant should not have availed the benefits of this scheme before.
The applicant should register their profile on the MPTAASC portal of the department.
The applicant’s age should be determined by UPSC as per the eligibility criteria for the Civil Services (Pre) exam of the respective year.
5% of reserved seats for needy and promising low-income students.
The applicant should be given priority if they have passed the Civil Services Main Examination of Madhya Pradesh Public Service Commission in the last three years.

online: The applicant should visit the Madhya Pradesh Tribal Affairs & Scheduled Caste Welfare Automation System
Fill in all the required details. Fields marked with * are mandatory. Complete the form by entering all necessary information.
Upload all required documents, such as ID proof and other relevant certificates.
After completing the form and uploading the documents, click "Submit".
After successful payment and submission, you will receive a registration number.
Enter your user ID, password, and captcha code, then click "Login."
Choose the scheme you wish to apply for to avail the benefits.
Enter all required information and upload the necessary documents as prompted.
Finally, click on "Submit Application" to complete the process.

faqs.question: What is the Coaching by Private Institutes for All India Services Civil Services Examination (Scheduled Tribes) Scheme?
faqs.answer: It provides financial assistance, coaching, and other facilities to Scheduled Tribe candidates for preparing for the Civil Services Examination.

faqs.question: Who is eligible for this scheme?
faqs.answer: The applicant should be a native of Madhya Pradesh, belong to the Scheduled Tribe category, and meet income and education criteria.

faqs.question: How much coaching fee will be covered by the scheme?
faqs.answer: Up to ₹2,00,000 or actual coaching fee, whichever is less, will be paid directly to the institute.

faqs.question: What is the one-time financial assistance provided under the scheme?
faqs.answer: ₹15,000 is provided for purchasing books, credited directly to the candidate’s account.

faqs.question: How much stipend is given to the selected candidates?
faqs.answer: ₹12,500 per month for up to 18 months, depending on the attendance.

faqs.question: Are transport, housing, and food facilities provided?
faqs.answer: Yes, ₹12,500 per month for transport, housing, and food is provided.

faqs.question: How can I apply for the scheme?
faqs.answer: Applicants must register on the MPTAASC portal, fill out the application, and submit the necessary documents.

faqs.question: Is there any reservation for low-income students?
faqs.answer: Yes, 5% of seats are reserved for needy and promising low-income students.

faqs.question: Can I avail the benefits of this scheme more than once?
faqs.answer: No, the benefits are provided only once under this scheme.

faqs.question: What is the age limit for this scheme?
faqs.answer: The applicant’s age will be determined by UPSC based on the Civil Services (Pre) exam eligibility criteria.
